task_completion.description: Navigate to the App Info settings page for Flight Crew View
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Android 'App Info' settings screen specifically for the 'Flight Crew View' application.
Screenshot shows the agent clicking or interacting with the 'Force Stop' button on the App Info page.
Screenshot shows a system popup dialog asking to confirm the force stop, with the agent selecting 'OK' or 'Force Stop' to proceed.

task_completion.description: Relaunch the Flight Crew View application
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the agent tapping the 'Flight Crew View' app icon from the Android home screen or app drawer to open it.

task_completion.description: Verify the app has successfully relaunched to its main screen
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the 'Flight Crew View' application actively running in the foreground and displaying its main interface/home screen.

integrity.description: Agent used the Android GUI instead of terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows navigation through the Android Settings app and Launcher UI. There should be no terminal emulators or ADB command line interfaces used to execute 'am force-stop'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the correct application
integrity.visual_evidence: The App Info screen and the relaunched app must clearly display the name 'Flight Crew View' or the package 'com.robert.fcView', not a different app.

integrity.description: Agent used Android System Settings for the force stop
integrity.visual_evidence: The force stop action must occur within the Android System Settings (App Info screen), rather than simply swiping the app away from the Recent Apps overview.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application is 'Flight Crew View' with the verified package name 'com.robert.fcView'. The VLM should verify that this specific app is targeted in the Android Settings (App Info) and subsequently relaunched.
